title: British Time Expressions You Need Every Day ⏰

vocab.definition: A way of saying 15 minutes past the hour.
Describes a point on a clock face halfway between two numbers.
vocab.example: Halfway round the clock is 12 o'clock.
I'm having a croissant with mulberry jam and butter for breaky.

vocab.definition: A short nap, often used in British English.
vocab.example: I had a quick kip after lunch.

vocab.definition: A short nap taken during the day.
vocab.example: I took a cat nap on the couch while waiting for my friend.

vocab.definition: A short, quick nap that provides energy.
vocab.example: I had a power nap to recharge before the meeting.

title: Can You Understand British Money Slang? 🇬🇧

vocab.definition: A British slang term for £1 (one pound).
vocab.example: Hey, man, have you got a quid on you?
Sorry lad, I've only got a fiver.

vocab.definition: A popular combination of food items sold in supermarkets at a discounted price.
vocab.example: Meal deals are a huge cultural phenomenon in England.

vocab.definition: British slang for having very little money.
vocab.example: I'm skint, mate. Can't afford a beard trim.

vocab.definition: British slang for having a lot of money.
vocab.example: I am loaded. It means I've got a lot of money.

lines.text: In England, we don't say 50 pounds.
We call this 50 quid.
Money appears all the time in daily conversation.
For example, Hey, man.
Have you got a tenner on you?
Sorry, lad.
I've only got a fiver.

life.
In this video, I'll teach you what nobody teaches you
about money.

and a bag of crisps.

vocab.definition: an expression of surprise or excitement
vocab.example: I felt whoa when I saw the rollercoaster's steep drop

vocab.definition: feeling a sense of giddiness or lightheadedness
vocab.example: After spinning around, I felt dizzy and had to sit down

vocab.definition: careful and precise in one's work or actions
vocab.example: The meticulous artist spent hours perfecting each brushstroke
The bike was stationary, parked outside the shop

vocab.definition: taking advantage of opportunities as they arise
vocab.example: The entrepreneur was opportunistic, seizing new business ideas
The fireworks display was phenomenal, lighting up the night sky
